details: The "Female Child Birth Gift Scheme," introduced by the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (HPBOCWWB), aims to provide financial support for the birth of daughters to registered beneficiaries. Under this scheme, the Board offers an amount of ₹51,000/- (fifty-one thousand) for each of two daughters, deposited in the form of a Fixed Deposit Receipt (FDR). This financial aid can only be withdrawn once the daughter turns 18, promoting long-term financial security for the girl child.
benefits: Financial support of ₹51,000/- per daughter (up to two)
eligibility: For Registration as a Building/Construction Worker:
The applicant should be a resident of Himachal Pradesh.
The applicant should be a Building or Construction Worker.
The age of the applicant should be between 18 and 60 years.
The applicant should have worked in building or construction activities for at least 90 days in the preceding 12 months.
The registration must be renewed annually.
Eligibility Criteria for Female Child Birth Gift Scheme:
The worker's membership with the Welfare Board must be active at the time of application.
The worker should have at least a two-month subscription to the Welfare Board.
The benefit is applicable for the birth of up to two daughters of the registered worker.

online: The Registration Process for Building and Other Construction Workers is as follows:
The interested applicant should visit the Labor Welfare Office (during office hours) and request a hard copy of the prescribed application form from the concerned authority.
, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste a passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attested, if required).

Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with a wage slip or appointment letter from the employer, verifying 90 days of construction work. If unavailable, a certificate from a local authority (such as a councilor, executive officer, or Panchayat Secretary) can be submitted to verify employment.
After submitting the documents and payment, the worker will receive an acknowledgment receipt confirming their registration.
Ensure that the application is submitted within the prescribed period, if any.
After three years, the worker must visit the office to pay a renewal fee and submit proof of 90 days of work each year to maintain eligibility for benefits.
A one-time registration fee must be paid at the office, and it will be valid for three years.

documents_required: Identity proof i.e. Aadhaar Card, Voter Card
Bank account details/copy of bank passbook

faqs.question: What is the Female Child Birth Gift Scheme?
faqs.answer: It is a financial assistance scheme by HPBOCWWB that provides ₹51,000 for each of two daughters of registered workers, deposited as a Fixed Deposit Receipt (FDR).

faqs.question: Who is eligible for this scheme?
faqs.answer: The worker must be engaged in construction work, registered with HPBOCWWB, and have an active membership with at least a two-month subscription.

faqs.question: How many daughters can receive this benefit?
faqs.answer: The scheme covers the birth of up to two daughters of a registered worker.

faqs.question: When can the financial assistance be withdrawn?
faqs.answer: The amount can be withdrawn only after the daughter turns 18, ensuring long-term financial security.

faqs.question: Is this benefit available for all workers?
faqs.answer: No, only workers registered under the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (HPBOCWWB) are eligible.

faqs.question: How can a worker register for HPBOCWWB?
faqs.answer: Workers can register by visiting the Labour Welfare Office in their jurisdiction or through self-registration on the official portal.

faqs.question: What is the age limit for registering with HPBOCWWB?
faqs.answer: The applicant must be between 18 and 60 years of age.

faqs.question: Is there a minimum work experience requirement?
faqs.answer: Yes, the worker must have completed at least 90 days of construction work.

faqs.question: Can the FDR be withdrawn before the daughter turns 18?
faqs.answer: No, the amount is locked in the Fixed Deposit Receipt (FDR) and can only be accessed once the daughter turns 18.

faqs.question: What happens if the worker’s membership becomes inactive?
faqs.answer: The worker must have an active membership at the time of application. An inactive membership may lead to rejection.
